tracks.meaning: a medium through which information or signals are transmitted, such as a television or radio station
a band of frequencies or a pathway through which data is transmitted in digital systems
a deep water passage or waterway suitable for navigation by ships
a natural watercourse such as a river, stream, or strait connecting two larger bodies of water
a tubular passage or duct in the body through which a substance flows, such as a blood vessel or nerve pathway
a groove or furrow cut into a surface to guide movement or direct flow
a distribution network or method through which products or services reach customers
to direct, guide, or steer something along a particular course or path
a deep underwater valley or depression in the ocean floor
a water conduit used to irrigate land or direct water flow for agricultural purposes
a passage or opening through which actors or scenery move on or off stage
to pursue or focus on a particular course of action or direction
